message:
fix(lua): correct select() to handle negative indices and reject zero index per Lua 5.1 spec

The built-in select() function previously only handled positive integer
indices and returned Nil for out-of-range values. This was incorrect
per the Lua 5.1 reference manual:

- select(0, ...) should raise an error ("index out of range")
- select(-N, ...) should count from the end of the argument list
  (e.g. select(-1, 'a', 'b', 'c') returns 'c')
- Non-finite or fractional indices should raise an error
- Positive indices beyond the argument count should return nothing
  (empty list), not Nil

Changes:
- Add input validation: reject non-finite, fractional, zero, and
  out-of-range negative indices with descriptive error messages
- Implement negative index resolution: -1 = last arg, -2 = second to
  last, etc., returning all args from that position onwards
- Return empty vec instead of Nil for positive indices past the end

Tests:
- select_negative_index_counts_from_tail: verifies select(-1, 'a', 'b', 'c')
  returns 'c'
- select_zero_index_errors: verifies select(0, ...) raises index error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
